title: Iterating Arrays 🔁
description: Combine Loops and Arrays to process huge amounts of data quickly.

slides.title: The Perfect Match 💍
slides.content:
# Loops + Arrays

Arrays hold huge lists of data. Loops allow us to run code repeatedly.

Combined, they are the most powerful tool in programming! We use loops to "iterate" (travel through, one by one) over every single item inside an array.

Imagine an array with 1,000 email addresses. We can write a loop to send an email to every single one in just 3 lines of code!

slides.title: The Classic For Loop 🤖
Total Health 💻
slides.content: See how a standard loop can quickly add up all numbers located inside an array:

slides.title: The Modern Way: forEach ✨

quizData.question: What is the primary benefit of using `myArray.forEach()` instead of a standard `for` loop?

options.text: It is much faster than a for loop
It does not require tracking indexes (like i) or checking the array's length
It can be used on plain text strings

quizData.explanation: Unlike the standard for loop, .forEach() handles all the messy indexing internally. It guarantees it will cleanly run your code exactly once for every item in the array.

slides.title: Another Great Option: map() 🗺️

quizData.question: Which method is specifically designed to transform an existing array and output a brand NEW modified array?
quizData.explanation: While .forEach() simply runs code, .map() specifically gathers all the returned values from that inner code and bundles them up into a fresh array to hand back to you.
